evidence_refs.quote: Lossy context compression for large language models risks discarding answer-critical information. We propose the Compression Gap Penalty (CGP), a lightweight text-only metric that quantifies semantic atom loss between a full context and its compressed form. CGP computes, for each fact atom in the original context, the maximum TF-IDF cosine similarity to any sentence in the compressed context, then reports the weighted mean unrecovered atom mass. We evaluate three weighting schemes—uniform, query
